prompt: What are top VCs (a16z, Sequoia, Greylock, Benchmark, Founders Fund, Index, etc.) and respected angels saying is the next big opportunity in AI for 2026-2027? Summarize their theses with direct quotes and sources. Cite.
content:
**Top VCs like a16z and Sequoia emphasize agentic AI (especially long-horizon agents), physical/embodied AI, AI infrastructure and data tooling, AI-native enterprise platforms (replacing legacy systems), and massive opportunities in coding and services as the key themes for 2026-2027.** These build on the shift from models to applications and execution, with agents positioned as a computational revolution akin to (or exceeding) the Industrial Revolution for cognitive work.[[1]](https://sequoiacap.com/article/ai-ascent-2026/)[[2]](https://sequoiacap.com/article/2026-this-is-agi/)

Other firms (Greylock, Benchmark, Founders Fund, Index) are active in AI but feature less public 2026-specific forward theses in recent coverage; themes overlap around vertical agents, infrastructure, and defensibility. Respected angels echo similar ideas around physical AI and agentic systems.[[3]](https://migratorg.substack.com/p/where-the-smart-money-goes-10-startup)[[4]](https://www.earthianai.com/news/articles/2026-03-05/best-ai-venture-capital-funds-2026-ranking)

### a16z (Andreessen Horowitz) Theses
a16z’s annual “Big Ideas 2026” series and related pieces highlight several concrete opportunities:

- **Unstructured/multimodal data platforms as a generational enterprise opportunity**: Enterprises need continuous cleaning, structuring, validation, and governance of multimodal data (documents, images, video) for AI workloads to succeed. Use cases span contracts, compliance, support, and agent workflows. “Untangling unstructured data becomes a generational opportunity.” 2026 is also “the year AI goes multimodal.”[[5]](https://a16z.com/newsletter/big-ideas-2026-part-1/)
- **AI coding as a ~$3 trillion opportunity** and broader AI apps maturation: Focus on tooling that moves beyond “making” to “thinking,” with faster enterprise diffusion of cheap code.[[6]](https://a16z.com/ai/)[[7]](https://a16z.com/notes-on-ai-apps-in-2026/)
- **Physical AI and the industrial stack**, AI-native platforms replacing legacy systems (e.g., in financial services/insurance, where the risk of *not* replacing legacy systems will exceed the risk of change by 2026), and humans focusing on ideas while AI handles execution.[[6]](https://a16z.com/ai/)[[8]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/angelastrange_new-2026-andreessen-horowitz-prediction-activity-7404548291356721154-C6MG)[[9]](https://a16z.com/humans-are-for-ideas-ai-is-for-execution/)

a16z has been the most active AI investor recently (25+ deals in 2025) and backs both infra and applications, including agent-related infrastructure.[[10]](https://www.tldl.io/resources/ai-investors-vcs-2026)

### Sequoia Capital Theses
Sequoia’s AI Ascent 2026 keynote and related pieces frame AI as a revolution in *computation* (not just communication), with 2026 as the pivotal year:

- **Long-horizon agents as the core opportunity (“This is AGI”)**: “Long-horizon agents are functionally AGI, and 2026 will be their year.” Agents will act as “doers” and “colleagues,” shifting usage from occasional to all-day/every-day. The three ingredients (models, tools, harnesses) have converged. This enables “whatever you could imagine building over the next 100 years... in 100 days.”[[2]](https://sequoiacap.com/article/2026-this-is-agi/)[[11]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LRo33rnv6rQ)
- **Cognitive revolution paralleling the Industrial Revolution**: AI will do to cognitive work what the Industrial Revolution did to manual labor. “Services is the new software,” with a TAM ~15x larger than traditional software. AI represents at least a $10 trillion opportunity (10x cloud computing).[[1]](https://sequoiacap.com/article/ai-ascent-2026/)[[12]](https://meditations.metavert.io/p/the-state-of-ai-agents-in-2026)
- Emphasis on agent-native workflows, vertical agents, and the gap between frontier model capabilities and enterprise deployment.[[13]](https://karpathy.bearblog.dev/sequoia-ascent-2026/)

Sequoia has backed major AI players (e.g., Databricks, Mistral, Hugging Face) and sees workflow-replacement/vertical AI as key.[[14]](https://www.sky9capital.com/blog/blog-ai-focused-investors-vc-firms-2026/)

### Other VCs and Broader Context
- **Physical/embodied AI and robotics infrastructure** appear as a recurring “next wave” theme across discussions (including a16z’s physical AI focus and broader investor commentary citing Jensen Huang on a potential $50T market). This includes sim-to-real execution, autonomous systems for industrial/high-risk environments, and supporting infra (power, data centers, minerals).[[6]](https://a16z.com/ai/)[[15]](https://x.com/heyshrutimishra/status/2034713616365363300)
- **Infrastructure, data efficiency, and agent tooling** (search for agents, growth agents, systems of intelligence vs. record) are highlighted in a16z investments and Sequoia framing.[[16]](https://x.com/a16z/status/2057140423706173726)
- Firms like Founders Fund (defense/American Dynamism AI), Greylock, Benchmark, and Index are active in AI but show fewer granular 2026 public theses in available sources; common threads include vertical AI, defensibility, and infrastructure.[[3]](https://migratorg.substack.com/p/where-the-smart-money-goes-10-startup)[[14]](https://www.sky9capital.com/blog/blog-ai-focused-investors-vc-firms-2026/)

**Respected angels/influencers** (e.g., via public commentary) align on physical AI as an overlooked massive opportunity beyond chat/LLM wrappers, with real-world deployment in 2026+ (robots in production environments).[[17]](https://x.com/Rewkang/status/1950073267802558745)

These views are drawn from firm publications, keynotes, and analyses as of mid-2026. Actual investment theses evolve with market data, and VCs often diversify across layers (infra to apps). For primary sources, see a16z’s Big Ideas series and Sequoia’s AI Ascent materials.
